# Why are there generated files in this repository?

The standard way to handle generated files in a go module is to put them directly in the repository. This is because go modules are just directories with go files in them, most commonly a git repository. There is no package step to generate the files, so they need to be present in the repository for users to be able to import them.

# How are the generated files generated?

They are generated by the Makefile with the `make protos` command. There is also a convenience script in `scripts/protos.sh`.

They will also be generated automatically when running the coverage script, the `coverage` target depends on the `protos` target, so running `make coverage` will also generate the protos. There is also a vscode task "Generate Protobufs" that runs the `protos.sh` script, and the launch configuration runs that task so the protos are generated automatically before debugging.

# What about updating them?

When the smpte submodule is updated, the generated files need to be updated as well. This will typically be done in a special PR just for updating the smpte submodule so large changes will be expected.
